expectations.text: Names the Checkpoint Test by name and applies it as the diagnostic tool (die, replay, ask whether patterns rearrange)
expectations.evidence: 'Run the Checkpoint Test: die and restart. Does the configuration feel like a new puzzle? If the same script dominates — if the player doesn't need to reassess priorities — the encounter still lacks meaningful prioritization choice.'

expectations.text: Identifies authoritative scripting / fixed-wave spawning as the anti-pattern causing the problem
expectations.evidence: 'Spawns are fully predetermined. Use percentage-based reinforcements — trigger new enemies when the current wave reaches a health/count threshold, not on a fixed timer or death count.'

expectations.text: Recommends encounter composition using orthogonally differentiated archetypes, not just 'add variety'
expectations.evidence: 'Compose with orthogonally differentiated archetypes: one immediate hitscan threat, one slow heavy, one fast flanker. Now the player must decide priority order each time.'

expectations.text: Explains Prioritization Choice as the goal: the player must decide which threat to address first with no obviously dominant answer
